sections.title: 6. Closing Thoughts: Fairness Beyond Functionality
path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
6. Closing Thoughts: Fairness Beyond Functionality

paragraphs.text: Dear Honoured Members of the European Commission,

As a citizen of the European Union who deeply values the guiding principles of fairness, consumer protection, technological progress, and the safeguarding of individual rights, I am writing with the utmost respect and sincere appreciation for your ongoing work on the proposed Digital Fairness Act. The European Union has long set global benchmarks in digital rights - through visionary regulations such as the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and the Digital Markets Act (DMA) - and this new initiative represents yet another crucial step toward ensuring that European consumers can engage in the digital economy with confidence, clarity, and fairness.

I welcome the stated goals of the Digital Fairness Act to protect consumers in digital environments, ensure greater transparency, prevent manipulative digital practices, and address asymmetries of power between consumers and large digital service providers. These objectives are more necessary than ever in our increasingly digitalised society, where the lines between product, service, and platform are often blurred - and where consumers frequently find themselves at the mercy of unilateral, unchallengeable decisions by corporations who control their access to purchased digital goods.

In this context, I wish to raise an urgent and growing concern among European citizens: the revocation of consumer access to purchased digital products, particularly software and video games, without due process, consent, or adequate legal remedies. This issue has gained significant traction in the form of a citizens' movement entitled "Stop Destroying Videogames" - an official European Citizens' Initiative that is now formally registered with the Commission. I humbly urge you to consider incorporating the spirit and the concrete proposals of this initiative into the scope and substance of the Digital Fairness Act.

What follows is a respectful and detailed elaboration of the concerns that underlie this request, and specific suggestions as to how the Digital Fairness Act can rise to this occasion - ensuring that the digital rights of consumers are fully protected, not only in their engagement with platforms and services, but in their ownership and continued use of digital products which they have lawfully acquired.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
paragraphs.text: The advent of digital distribution has revolutionised access to software, video games, eBooks, and many other forms of creative and interactive media. While these changes have brought undeniable convenience and diversity to consumers, they have also fundamentally disrupted traditional understandings of ownership.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
paragraphs.text: Where once consumers could buy a physical product - such as a game cartridge, DVD, or CD-ROM - and retain access to it indefinitely, today's digital transactions increasingly come with severe and often hidden limitations. These include:

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
paragraphs.text: Dependency on Online Authentication: Many games and software titles now require ongoing verification through remote servers, even in single-player or offline modes. If these servers are shut down, consumers lose access to the software they have legally purchased.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
paragraphs.text: De-listing and Deactivation: Products can be "de-listed" from platforms such as Steam, PlayStation Store, or Xbox Live, rendering them unavailable for purchase - and sometimes even for re-download - without notice to consumers.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
paragraphs.text: Digital Rights Management (DRM): While DRM is intended to prevent piracy, in practice it often limits legitimate users from accessing or backing up their purchases. Moreover, DRM mechanisms are rarely disclosed transparently before purchase.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
paragraphs.text: Unilateral Revocation: Companies may terminate user accounts, revoke licenses, or disable functionality - frequently without due process or any effective appeal mechanism.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
paragraphs.text: These practices not only erode consumer confidence and disrupt long-term cultural preservation, but they violate the basic principles of contractual certainty and fairness, which EU law traditionally upholds. The Digital Fairness Act is uniquely positioned to address this imbalance.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
2. Why Video games Matter: Beyond Entertainment
paragraphs.text: While some may view video games as a niche or purely recreational category, this perspective underestimates both their economic significance and their cultural and artistic value. The European video game sector generates billions of euros annually, supports thousands of creative professionals, and engages over 50% of EU citizens, particularly among younger demographics.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
2. Why Video games Matter: Beyond Entertainment
paragraphs.text: Video games also serve educational, narrative, and artistic functions; they are increasingly recognised as cultural artefacts worthy of preservation. Yet, paradoxically, many games become irretrievable or non-functional within years of their release due to corporate decisions, lack of preservation mechanisms, or DRM obsolescence.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
2. Why Video games Matter: Beyond Entertainment
paragraphs.text: As the "Stop Destroying Videogames" initiative rightly asserts, we are witnessing a form of digital cultural erosion - whereby interactive works disappear from public reach, not because of piracy or obsolescence, but because of commercial neglect or active destruction.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
2. Why Video games Matter: Beyond Entertainment
paragraphs.text: The European Union has already recognised the cultural value of video games in its Creative Europe funding, and the Digital Fairness Act should reflect this by safeguarding access to these cultural goods post-purchase.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
3. Incorporating the Principles of the "Stop Destroying Videogames" Initiative
paragraphs.text: The Citizens' Initiative "Stop Destroying Videogames" calls for concrete and reasonable actions to ensure long-term access to legally acquired video games. Among its proposals are:

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
3. Incorporating the Principles of the "Stop Destroying Videogames" Initiative
paragraphs.text: Obligations for Publishers to Preserve Functionality: If a game is removed from sale or its online services are discontinued, consumers should still be able to access and play any single-player or offline components they have purchased.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
3. Incorporating the Principles of the "Stop Destroying Videogames" Initiative
paragraphs.text: Mandatory Offline Modes: For single-player games, publishers should provide mechanisms to play without needing online authentication, especially after the product's commercial lifecycle has ended.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
3. Incorporating the Principles of the "Stop Destroying Videogames" Initiative
paragraphs.text: Preservation Rights for Cultural Institutions: Libraries, archives, and museums should have the legal right to preserve and emulate digital games for cultural purposes.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
3. Incorporating the Principles of the "Stop Destroying Videogames" Initiative
paragraphs.text: Transparency at Point of Sale: Consumers should be clearly informed if a digital product is dependent on servers, DRM, or third-party services, and what conditions might limit future access.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
3. Incorporating the Principles of the "Stop Destroying Videogames" Initiative
paragraphs.text: All of these requests align closely with the broader goals of the Digital Fairness Act: to ensure that consumers have clear, truthful information, and are protected from deceptive or unfair business practices.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
3. Incorporating the Principles of the "Stop Destroying Videogames" Initiative
paragraphs.text: Incorporating these ideas would not only respond to democratic input from EU citizens but would also demonstrate that the EU is serious about protecting both consumer rights and digital culture.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
4. Suggestions for Concrete Legislative Measures
paragraphs.text: To address these concerns and protect digital consumers, I respectfully propose the following elements for inclusion in the final form of the Digital Fairness Act:

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
4. Suggestions for Concrete Legislative Measures
a) Right to Continued Access
paragraphs.text: A clear and enforceable "right to use" should be established for all digital goods legally acquired by consumers. This right should include:

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
4. Suggestions for Concrete Legislative Measures
a) Right to Continued Access
paragraphs.text: Access to the product in its essential form even after commercial support ends.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
4. Suggestions for Concrete Legislative Measures
a) Right to Continued Access
paragraphs.text: The right to back up and locally store digital purchases.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
4. Suggestions for Concrete Legislative Measures
a) Right to Continued Access
paragraphs.text: Protection against account-based revocation unless a serious breach of contract occurs.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
b) Obligations for Transparency and Disclosures
paragraphs.text: Digital marketplaces and platforms must clearly inform consumers about:

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
b) Obligations for Transparency and Disclosures
paragraphs.text: The technical and legal conditions under which a product may become unusable.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
b) Obligations for Transparency and Disclosures
paragraphs.text: The nature of DRM restrictions and online dependencies.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
b) Obligations for Transparency and Disclosures
paragraphs.text: Any planned end-of-life dates for online services at the time of purchase.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
b) Obligations for Transparency and Disclosures
paragraphs.text: These disclosures should be provided in plain language and in a standardised format across platforms.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
c) Server Sunset Obligations
paragraphs.text: When publishers discontinue online services that are essential to accessing or playing a product, they should be required to:

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
c) Server Sunset Obligations
paragraphs.text: Offer an offline patch or alternative method to preserve functionality.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
c) Server Sunset Obligations
paragraphs.text: Transfer server code to trusted preservation institutions, where appropriate.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
c) Server Sunset Obligations
paragraphs.text: Provide adequate notice and options to consumers before deactivation.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
d) Right to Repair and Modify for Continued Access
paragraphs.text: Much as hardware owners have the right to repair their devices, software owners should have the right to bypass DRM or modify executables solely for the purpose of continuing to use the product they purchased, particularly when commercial support is withdrawn.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
d) Right to Repair and Modify for Continued Access
paragraphs.text: This could be coupled with exceptions in copyright law for preservation, personal use, and academic research.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
5. A Matter of Trust and Democratic Legitimacy
paragraphs.text: The success of the Digital Fairness Act - and indeed, of the digital single market more broadly - depends on the trust and confidence of European consumers. When individuals pay for a product, they should not have to fear that it will be taken away from them arbitrarily or become unusable due to circumstances beyond their control.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
5. A Matter of Trust and Democratic Legitimacy
paragraphs.text: By reinforcing the rights of digital ownership, the EU can stand apart from jurisdictions where consumer rights are frequently subordinated to corporate convenience. Moreover, incorporating feedback from official Citizens' Initiatives such as "Stop Destroying Videogames" will show that democratic participation remains a living and impactful part of the EU's legislative process.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
6. Closing Thoughts: Fairness Beyond Functionality
paragraphs.text: Digital fairness must go beyond mere usability. It must reflect the principles of contractual good faith, transparency, cultural stewardship, and consumer dignity. In the digital realm, where access is controlled through code, servers, and licenses, the balance of power tilts too easily in favour of publishers and platforms. Legislation must restore that balance.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
6. Closing Thoughts: Fairness Beyond Functionality
paragraphs.text: As an engaged EU citizen, I am confident that the Commission shares these values and that the final Digital Fairness Act will reflect the will of the people as well as the spirit of European law. I express my sincere thanks to all policymakers and staff who are working diligently to create a fairer digital future for all.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
6. Closing Thoughts: Fairness Beyond Functionality
paragraphs.text: Thank you kindly for your attention to this letter, and for your enduring commitment to the rights and well-being of European consumers.

section_path: 1. The Growing Fragility of Digital Ownership
6. Closing Thoughts: Fairness Beyond Functionality
paragraphs.text: With highest respect and hope, a Concerned and Engaged EU Citizen.
